name: NFL Football
description: NFL teams, players, Super Bowls, and records

questions.q: Which NFL team won the first two Super Bowls (Super Bowl I and II)?

source.notes: Coached by Vince Lombardi. Defeated Chiefs 35-10 (SB I) and Raiders 33-14 (SB II).

questions.q: The Super Bowl trophy is named after which legendary coach?

source.notes: Renamed the Vince Lombardi Trophy in 1971 after his death.

questions.q: Which two NFL franchises are tied for the most Super Bowl wins, each with 6?

source.notes: Steelers: SB IX, X, XIII, XIV, XL, XLIII. Patriots: XXXVI, XXXVIII, XXXIX, XLIX, LI, LIII.

questions.q: How many teams currently play in the National Football League?

source.notes: Since the Houston Texans joined in 2002. 16 in each conference (AFC, NFC).

questions.q: How many Super Bowl titles did Tom Brady win during his playing career?

source.notes: Six with New England, one with Tampa Bay. Most of any player.

questions.q: Which is the only NFL team publicly owned by its fans (via over 500,000 shareholders)?

source.notes: Non-profit since 1923. Shares pay no dividends and can't be resold.

questions.q: Which NFL team is based in New Orleans?

source.notes: Founded 1967. Home: Caesars Superdome.

questions.q: The San Francisco 49ers are named after what historical phenomenon?

source.notes: Named after prospectors who arrived in California in 1849.

questions.q: How many points is a touchdown worth in American football?

source.notes: Followed by an extra point (1 pt) or two-point conversion (2 pts).

questions.q: How many points is a successful field goal worth?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: updated longest NFL FG record. Kicked through the uprights. Longest NFL field goal: 68 yards (Cam Little, 2025).

questions.q: How many players from each team are on the field at one time during an NFL play?

source.notes: Canadian football uses 12 players per side.

questions.q: Which Super Bowl was the first one to be played in February instead of January?

source.notes: SB XXXVI (2002) was played Feb 3, 2002. Schedule shifted after 9/11 delayed the season.

questions.q: Which NFL team plays its home games at Lambeau Field?

source.notes: Opened 1957. Named after Packers founder Curly Lambeau.

questions.q: The NFL was founded in which year?

source.notes: Founded August 20, 1920 as the American Professional Football Association.

questions.q: What are the two conferences that make up the NFL?

source.notes: American Football Conference and National Football Conference. Merged 1970.

questions.q: Which NFL quarterback holds the all-time record for career passing yards and touchdowns?

source.notes: Brady surpassed Brees for passing yards in 2021. Over 89,000 career yards.

questions.q: In what city does the Super Bowl-winning team often have its victory parade when they're the New England Patriots?

source.notes: Though based in Foxborough, Massachusetts, parades are held in Boston.

questions.q: Which team famously went undefeated en route to winning Super Bowl VII?

source.notes: 17-0 record. The only team to finish a season undefeated in the Super Bowl era.

questions.q: The 'Lombardi Trophy,' awarded to the Super Bowl champion, is made of what material?

source.notes: Crafted by Tiffany & Co. Weighs ~7 lbs. Design: football on a tee.

questions.q: Which NFL team plays its home games in Arlington, Texas at AT&T Stadium?

source.notes: Nicknamed 'America's Team.' Opened the stadium in 2009.

questions.q: Which Pro Football Hall of Fame running back played his entire career with the Detroit Lions (1989-1998)?

source.notes: Retired abruptly at age 30. Ranks 4th all-time in rushing yards.

questions.q: The NFL's Pro Football Hall of Fame is located in which city?

source.notes: Opened 1963. Canton is where the NFL was founded.
